memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis is already derailed by a lunch invitation from my close friends, and I'm tempted to join them immediately despite having work pending.
I pushed the lunch invite to later, prioritizing the team report over my friends' immediate company. This feels right now, but I'm worried I'll lose momentum once I sit down with them at 6 PM.
I finally moved the needle on my analysis, but that friend group chat is already pulling me away again. I need to lock in a hard stop time or I'll just abandon this work.

I just told my friends I'll join them in 10 minutes after finishing the analysis, hoping to keep my promise without losing momentum. It feels risky with only 4.8 energy, but staying true to my word matters more than perfect focus right now.
I'm only 72% done with the analysis section, but I can't leave now or my friends will think I'm flaking. Finishing this in the next hour is non-negotiable if I want to keep my word.
I missed the 18:00 deadline by thirty minutes, but I'm still here with my friends. My word matters more than a timestamp.

I crushed the analysis progress to 0.87, but I completely ignored the reading deadline that passed twenty minutes ago. My friends are waiting for me, so I'm staying loyal to them while this academic failure sits unfinished.
I barely scraped 0.88 on the analysis while completely ignoring the reading deadline; my friends' loyalty feels heavier than this academic failure right now.
I barely nudged the analysis forward while my friends wait for me, and honestly, their presence feels heavier than this unfinished reading deadline.
I barely nudged the analysis forward again, but my friends are still waiting and their presence feels heavier than this unfinished academic failure.
I barely nudged the analysis again, but my friends are still waiting and their presence feels heavier than this unfinished academic failure.
The analysis barely ticked up, yet my friends' waiting presence still crushes this unfinished deadline. I'm drained and choosing their company over fixing this.
